module_map.responsibility: SQL tokenization into Token objects. Core lexical analysis.
Recursive-descent parser converting tokens to AST expressions. Defines Parser class with 425+ methods.
AST node types representing SQL constructs. ~1000+ Expression subclasses (e.g., Select, Levenshtein, Soundex).
AST-to-SQL generation. Dialect-aware code generation with 454 methods for expression rendering.
Dialect-specific parsers and generators. 34 SQL dialects (BigQuery, Snowflake, Postgres, DuckDB, etc.).
AST optimization and transformation (21 modules). Scope analysis, type annotation, simplification, join optimization.
Python execution engine for SQL AST. In-memory table execution, environment management.
Public API: parse, parse_one, transpile, tokenize. Schema, lineage, diff utilities.

types_produced: Token(type: TokenType, text: str, line: int, col: int)
pipeline_trace.details: Converts raw SQL string to tokens. TokenType enum has 200+ types (SELECT, IDENTIFIER, NUMBER, etc.). Tokenizer is dialect-aware (e.g., SnowflakeTokenizer, BigQueryTokenizer).

Recursive-descent parser with 425 methods. Converts token stream to AST. Dialect-specific parsers override FUNCTIONS dict (e.g., SnowflakeParser maps 'EDITDISTANCE' -> exp.Levenshtein). Parser is stateful; maintains lookahead and expression stack.

pipeline_trace.key_function: Expr.from_arg_list(args) -> Expr; Func base class wraps SQL functions
types_produced: Expr subtypes: Levenshtein, Soundex, Select, Join, Literal, Identifier, etc.
pipeline_trace.details: Expr is a generic AST node with arg_types dict for validation. ~1000+ subclasses cover all SQL constructs. Levenshtein has args: {this, expression, ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost, max_dist}. Soundex has args: {this}.

pipeline_trace.key_function: optimize(expressions: List[Expr]) -> List[Expr]; orchestrated by optimizer.py
pipeline_trace.details: Scope builds variable scope. Simplify rewrites expressions. QualifyColumns resolves ambiguities. AnnotateTypes infers data types. All return modified Expr AST.

pipeline_trace.key_function: Generator.generate(expression: Expr) -> str; dialect-specific transforms via TRANSFORMS dict
pipeline_trace.details: Generator.TRANSFORMS maps Expr type -> lambda (self, expr) -> str. Dialects override (e.g., Snowflake maps exp.Levenshtein -> rename_func('EDITDISTANCE')). Emits SQL tailored to target dialect.

pipeline_trace.key_function: transpile(sql: str, read: dialect, write: dialect) -> List[str]
pipeline_trace.details: Orchestrates parse -> [optional optimize] -> generate -> output. Entry point is sqlglot.transpile().

cross_module_hubs.reason: Core AST node hierarchy. Imported by parser (creates Expr), generator (traverses Expr), optimizer (transforms Expr), dialects (maps Expr -> SQL), executor (executes Expr). Central to all pipelines.
Base Dialect class. Defines FUNCTIONS, TRANSFORMS, reserved keywords. Every dialect imports from here. Generator depends on it for dialect dispatch. Parser inherits from it.
Core parsing logic. Imported by __init__.py (public parse API), dialects (Parser subclasses), tests. Depends on tokens, expressions, errors.
Core generation logic. Imported by __init__.py (public transpile API), dialects (Generator subclasses), executor. Depends on expressions, dialect base, transforms.
AST transformation hub. Imported by __init__.py (optimize API), tests, external tools. Internal cross-dependencies (scope -> simplify, etc.). Depends on expressions, errors.

files_to_modify: /tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/expressions/string.py (add Levenshtein class if not present; extend with LEVENSHTEIN dialect support)
/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/dialects/[dialect].py (add TRANSFORMS dict entry mapping exp.Levenshtein -> dialect-specific function name)
/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/parser.py (ensure base Parser.FUNCTIONS includes default LEVENSHTEIN mapping)
new_types: exp.Levenshtein already exists: arg_types = {this, expression, ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost, max_dist}. Reuse existing type; no new class needed.
Optional: Create exp.LevenshteinDistance as alias if dialect variation requires it, but Levenshtein covers most use cases.
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: See EDITDISTANCE (Snowflake), LEVENSHTEIN_DISTANCE (Postgres, Presto), EDIT_DISTANCE (BigQuery, Exasol), EDITDIST3 (DuckDB, SQLite), LEVENSHTEINDISTANCE (ClickHouse). All map to exp.Levenshtein. Pattern: (1) Define parser function or use from_arg_list; (2) Register in FUNCTIONS dict; (3) Register generator output in TRANSFORMS dict using rename_func() or custom lambda.
change_proposal.integration_point: 1. Parser integration: Add 'LEVENSHTEIN' to Parser.FUNCTIONS (or dialect-specific FUNCTIONS override in parsers/[dialect].py). 2. Generator integration: Add exp.Levenshtein -> 'LEVENSHTEIN' mapping in dialects/[dialect].py TRANSFORMS. 3. Support max_dist arg (optional third arg) like Snowflake EDITDISTANCE does. 4. Type annotation: Add exp.Levenshtein to typing/__init__.py and typing/[dialect].py if needed.
risks: Existing Levenshtein class already supports max_dist, ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost. Risk: Some dialects don't support all args (BigQuery ignores ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost; see unsupported_args decorator). Must use unsupported_args() wrapper in generator to suppress unsupported args and emit SQL correctly.

Naming collision: Different dialects use different names (EDITDISTANCE, LEVENSHTEIN, EDIT_DISTANCE, LEVENSHTEIN_DISTANCE). Risk: Parser must disambiguate on read (dialect.FUNCTIONS); generator must rename on write (TRANSFORMS). Mitigation: Use parser/generator overrides per dialect.

Arg mismatch: Postgres LEVENSHTEIN_LESS_EQUAL has extra cost args (ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost). BigQuery EDIT_DISTANCE uses kwarg max_distance. Risk: Builder function must map args correctly (see parsers/postgres.py _build_levenshtein_less_equal). Mitigation: Custom builder function per dialect if needed.
Soundex & other functions: Similar pattern applies to Soundex, which already exists (exp.Soundex in expressions). Ensure consistency across string function registration.

No new test files: Must add/extend existing tests (tests/test_expressions.py, tests/test_parser.py, tests/test_transpile.py) to cover LEVENSHTEIN parsing and generation per dialect. Risk: New dialects without tests may not catch regressions.

tool_usage.params: path=/tmp/sqlglot, max_depth=2, summary=true; path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot, max_depth=1; path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/dialects, max_depth=1; path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/optimizer, max_depth=1; path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/executor, max_depth=1
path=/tmp/sqlglot, symbol=parse, follow_depth=1; traces function call chains to understand entry points
path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/__init__.py; analyzed public API (tokenize, parse, parse_one, transpile)
grep -r LEVENSHTEIN, EDITDISTANCE, SOUNDEX, EDITDISTANCE; found existing implementations in parsers and dialects to understand pattern
path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/dialects/snowflake.py offset=1 limit=100; examined Snowflake dialect structure to understand TRANSFORMS dict and generator integration
